text:
I'll search for this paper to verify the cited title and authors.
The web search confirms that the cited reference is accurate. Multiple sources document that 
James J. Heckman and Richard Robb Jr. published "Alternative methods for evaluating the impact of interventions: An overview" in the Journal of Econometrics in 1985, volume 30(1-2), pages 239-267
.

The automated checker reported metadata mismatches, but this appears to be an error in the checker's matching process. The ScienceDirect entry (result 1) and the RePEc database entry (result 2) both clearly show the paper with the exact cited title, authors, venue, and year. The "R. Rohh" and year 2001 mentioned by the automated checker do not match any legitimate version of this paper found in the search results. The Semantic Scholar entry citing "R. Rohh" (result 5) appears to be a metadata error on that platform, not an accurate representation of the paper.

The search results from multiple authoritative sources (ScienceDirect, RePEc, EconPapers, Cambridge University Press, and NCBI) all confirm the cited reference is correct: Heckman and Robb, 1985, published in the Journal of Econometrics.

EXPLANATION: The exact paper was found in multiple authoritative academic sources, confirming the cited title, authors, and publication details are accurate.
LINK: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/0304407685901393
FOUND_TITLE: Alternative methods for evaluating the impact of interventions: An overview
FOUND_AUTHORS: James J. Heckman, Richard Robb Jr.
FOUND_YEAR: 1985
VERDICT: UNLIKELY
